content.full_text:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice on behalf of the Gun Owners
of California Campaign Commiffee regarding the creation of a small contributor
committee under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r
F.q,rn Polrrrcel Pnacrrcps CouvrrssroN
P.O. Box 807 .428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
March 30,2001
David Bauer
400 Capitol Mall, Suite 1560
Sacramento, CA 95814
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-01-042
Dear Mr. Bauer:
QUESTTONS
May the existing committee become a small contributor committee by
creating a separate bank account and segregating any funds in excess of
$200 per year from a single source into that account for non-candidate
support activities?
If a contribution of $200 is received from an individual and placed into the
small contributor committee, may additional contributions received from
the same contributor be placed into a general purpose recipient
committee?
CONCLUSIONS
No. Assuming that the committee meets all other criteria for qualification,
a small contributor committee's campaign funds may not contain
contributions in excess of $200 from a single source in a calendar year.
t 
Gorernment Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear attitle 2, sections
18109 - 18995, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.
1
2
1


Bauer, David
Page Two
A-}t-042
2.
However, funds in excess of $200 per contributor may be transferred to a
new cornmittee. If all the other criteria are met, the existing committee
may then amend its Statement of Organization, Form 410, to indicate that
it is a small contributor committee. After the small contributor committee
has been established, it may contribute up to $6,000 per election to any
one state legislative candidate. If contributions will be made to a state
legislative candidate from both a general purpose committee and a small
contributor committee, the total contribution may not exceed $6,000, and
no more than $3,000 may be contributed from the general purpose
committee.
Yes. Any additional contributions received from the same contributor
may be placed in the general purpose committee. If a contribution in
excess of $200 is received from one source, the entire contribution should
be placed into the account for the general purpose committee, after which
up to $200 may be transferred to the small contributor committee. Under
no circumstances should a contribution of more than $200 from a single
source be placed in the small contributor committee account.
FACTS
The Gun Owners of California Campaign Committee is a general purpose
recipient committee that receives its funding from a large base of contributors. While
most contributions received have been less than $200, a few contributions have exceeded
that amount.
ANALYSIS
Section 85203 defines a "small conkibutor committee" as:
"...any committee that meets all of the following criteria:
(a) The committee has been in existence for at least six months.
(b) The committee receives contributions from 100 or more
persons.
(c) No one person has contributed to the committee more than two
hundred dollars (5200) per calendar year.
(d) The committee makes contributions to five or more
candidates."


Bauer, David
Page Three
A-01-042
Regulation 18503 provides that "the committee may count the length of time it
existed before January 1,2001, to meet the six month requirement." In addition,
Regulation 18503(a)(3) states that "the committee's campaign funds [may] not include
contributions of more than $200 made in a calendar year by any person."2 Therefore, by
transferring those amounts that exceed the $200limit from the existing committee to
another committee, a:ld assuming the existing committee has been in existence for at least
six months, the committee will meet criteria (a) and (c) listed above.
In addition, nothing in the Act prohibits the Gun Owners Campaign Committee
from receiving additional contributions from conkibutors to the small contributor
committee and placing those funds into a separate general purpose committee. In order
to preserve the small contributor committee's status under subsection (a)(3) of Regulation
18503, contributor checks received that will be divided between the two committees
should not be placed in the small contributor committee account if they exceed $200, but
should be deposited into the general purpose committee account first.
Please note that Section 85301 prohibits contributions by any person, including a
general purpose committee, of more than $3,000 to any one candidate for state legislative
office per election, except for small contributor committees, whose limit is $6,000.
(Section 85302.) The Act does not limit contributions to local candidates or candidates
for statewide office.
In addition, Section 85311(c) states that "if two or more entities make
contributions that are directed and controlled by a majority of the same persons, the
contributions of those entities shall be aggregated." Assuming that the proposed small
contributor committee and the general purpose committee will be controlled by a
majority of the same persons, the two political action committees will be considered
affiliated entities. Therefore, the contributions made by the two committees will be
aggregated for purposes of contribution limits to state legislative candidates.
' 
Rtgulation 18503 was adopted in final form on March g,2OOl. Section (aX3) has been reworded
to provide "The committee's campaign funds do not include any contributions that exceed $200 per person
per calendar year, including campaign funds received before January l, 2001." This does not change the
conclusion in this letter.


Bauer, David
Page Four
A-01-042
If you have additional questions, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5660
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
By:
Moen
Political Reform Consultant II
